multimodal-shared-embeddings.claim_text: Shared embedding spaces between natural language and biological sequences enable cross-modal retrieval, sequence-grounded question answering, and unified inference across literature and experimental data.

candidates.paper_title: Prot2Text: Multimodal Protein's Function Generation with GNNs and Transformers

useful_outcomes.rationale: Prot2Text learns aligned protein-text representations enabling free-text function generation, directly demonstrating cross-modal mapping.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open encoder-decoder recipe pairing GNN protein encoder with LLM text decoder; readily adapted to DNA.

plausible.rationale: Clean empirical methodology with quantitative comparison; well-grounded application of GNN+LLM.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on SwissProt-scale proteins; scales with encoders but not yet to genome-scale DNA.

how_to_validate.rationale: BLEU/ROUGE plus retrieval metrics on text-protein pairs offer a transferable validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: A Systematic Study of Joint Representation Learning on Protein Sequences and Structures

useful_outcomes.rationale: Joint sequence+structure protein representations show empirical gains; analogous to text-sequence joint learning but text modality absent.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Methods (contrastive joint encoders) directly transferable to text-DNA shared embedding teams.

plausible.rationale: Systematic empirical comparison of joint-representation strategies; sound methodology.

scalable.rationale: Protein-scale; technique generalizes but not directly demonstrated at genome+literature scale.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides benchmark function-prediction tasks and transfer evaluation transferable to multimodal validation.

candidates.paper_title: S-PLM: Structure-aware Protein Language Model via Contrastive Learning between Sequence and Structure

useful_outcomes.rationale: S-PLM uses contrastive learning to align protein sequence and structure embeddings; methodology mirrors text-sequence CLIP-style alignment.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open contrastive-alignment recipe directly adaptable for text-DNA shared embeddings today.

plausible.rationale: Sound contrastive design with downstream validation; methodology is a clear template.

scalable.rationale: Protein scale demonstrated; contrastive framework scales but text-corpus pairing not addressed here.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides multi-task downstream benchmarks and zero-shot retrieval analogues that transfer.

candidates.paper_title: Scientific Large Language Models: A Survey on Biological & Chemical Domains

useful_outcomes.rationale: Survey systematically catalogs scientific LLMs spanning text plus DNA/protein/molecule modalities, including shared-space approaches.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides immediately useful taxonomy and references for teams building text-genomic shared embeddings.

plausible.rationale: Recent comprehensive survey citing established methods; methodology of survey itself is sound.

scalable.rationale: Conceptual mapping covers genome-scale and literature-scale systems but does not itself scale anything.

how_to_validate.rationale: Aggregates evaluation strategies across cited works that transfer to multimodal embedding validation.

candidates.paper_title: The STRING database in 2023: protein–protein association networks and functional enrichment analyses for any sequenced genome of interest

useful_outcomes.rationale: STRING aggregates literature-mined and experimental PPIs but does not learn a shared text-sequence embedding space.

immediate_benefit.rationale: STRING is widely used as a literature-grounded annotation source feeding multimodal pipelines, but provides databases not embeddings.

plausible.rationale: Mature, peer-reviewed integration of text-mined and experimental evidence; methodology is sound for its stated goal.

scalable.rationale: Operates across all sequenced genomes, demonstrating literature-scale text aggregation paired with sequence identifiers.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides confidence scoring and benchmark networks transferrable as supervision targets, but no embedding-space evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Beyond the Imitation Game: Quantifying and extrapolating the capabilities of language models

useful_outcomes.rationale: BIG-bench evaluates LLM capabilities broadly; some bio tasks but no shared sequence-text embedding space.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides task suite usable to evaluate text-only LLMs but not embedding-space alignment with sequences.

plausible.rationale: Large, peer-contributed benchmark with rigorous methodology for capability extrapolation.

scalable.rationale: 204+ tasks across many models; demonstrates literature-scale benchmarking infrastructure.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation methodology useful as a template but not specific to cross-modal retrieval evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Mapping single-cell data to reference atlases by transfer learning

useful_outcomes.rationale: scArches maps single-cell data to reference atlases via transfer learning; no language modality involvement.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful for single-cell embedding alignment but does not provide text-sequence shared embeddings.

plausible.rationale: Architectural-surgery transfer-learning approach is sound and well validated in single-cell domain.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated on multi-million cell atlases; scalable embedding architecture but not multimodal with text.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides reference-mapping benchmarks and label-transfer evaluation transferable as a methodology template.

candidates.paper_title: Modeling polypharmacy side effects with graph convolutional networks

useful_outcomes.rationale: Graph-convolutional drug-drug side-effect prediction; lacks any natural-language modality grounding biological sequences.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful for graph-based drug interaction tasks but offers no text-sequence shared embedding usable today.

plausible.rationale: Graph convolution over multimodal molecular graphs is methodologically sound for the side-effect task it tackles.

scalable.rationale: Scales to large drug-drug graphs but not to genome-scale sequences with literature-scale text.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides AUROC validation on side-effect prediction, transferable in spirit but not to text-DNA embeddings.

candidates.paper_title: Are LLMs Ready for Real-World Materials Discovery?

useful_outcomes.rationale: Position paper on LLM limits in materials science; no shared text-sequence embeddings demonstrated.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Discusses gaps and frameworks; offers little adoptable methodology for text-genomic embedding teams.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable analysis of LLM failure cases backed by examples; not an empirical contribution.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling concerns conceptually; no concrete scalable method proposed.

how_to_validate.rationale: Suggests qualitative failure-case probing transferable as a stress-test methodology.

candidates.paper_title: A Review of Large Language Models and Autonomous Agents in Chemistry

useful_outcomes.rationale: Review of LLM agents in chemistry; tangential to text-DNA shared embedding spaces.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Offers tooling references but no concrete shared embedding architecture.

plausible.rationale: Review methodology is reasonable; no empirical claims of its own.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scalable agentic frameworks but not embedding scalability in genomics.

how_to_validate.rationale: Aggregates evaluation patterns from LLM-agent literature transferable indirectly.

multimodal-instruction-tuning.claim_text: Instruction tuning lets language models follow user queries that reference DNA / RNA sequences, condition on sequence inputs, and produce structured biological reasoning grounded in the input sequence.

candidates.paper_title: GeneGPT: augmenting large language models with domain tools for improved access to biomedical information

useful_outcomes.rationale: GeneGPT instructs Codex to call NCBI APIs and reason over genomic queries, achieving 0.83 on GeneTuring; direct sequence-grounded instruction following.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open code, in-context demonstrations, and decoding algorithm immediately deployable for sequence-aware QA.

plausible.rationale: Sound use of API-augmented decoding with empirical state-of-the-art results.

scalable.rationale: Web API access scales across NCBI's literature- and genome-scale resources.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides GeneTuring + GeneHop benchmarks specifically for sequence/genomic instruction validation, directly transferable.

candidates.paper_title: Leveraging large language models for predictive chemistry

useful_outcomes.rationale: Shows fine-tuning GPT-3 to follow chemistry prompts beats specialized ML; direct evidence instruction tuning works on molecular sequences.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides a template recipe for prompt-formatted sequence questions plus fine-tuning, immediately portable to DNA/RNA.

plausible.rationale: Empirically grounded across multiple chemistry tasks with comparison baselines; sound methodology.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated at GPT-3 scale on molecular text; scaling to nucleotide context is plausible but not shown.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides multi-task benchmark protocols comparing instruction-tuned vs specialized models, directly transferable.

candidates.paper_title: LLaVA-Med: Training a Large Language-and-Vision Assistant for Biomedicine in One Day

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates fast visual-instruction tuning for biomedical images; analogous template for sequence-conditioned instruction tuning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open instruction-tuning recipe (curriculum, GPT-4 generated instructions) directly portable to DNA/RNA modalities.

plausible.rationale: Curriculum learning + LLaVA architecture is sound and well documented.

scalable.rationale: Trained in one day with modest compute; methodology scales easily to larger sequence corpora.

how_to_validate.rationale: Open-ended VQA evaluation paradigm transfers cleanly to sequence-instruction QA validation.

candidates.paper_title: Adapted large language models can outperform medical experts in clinical text summarization

useful_outcomes.rationale: Demonstrates instruction-adapted LLMs outperform clinicians at clinical text summarization; sequence inputs absent.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Concrete instruction-tuning recipes (prompt + adapter) with strong physician-evaluated outcomes, adoptable today.

plausible.rationale: Rigorous head-to-head clinical reader study with statistical analysis; methodology is exemplary.

scalable.rationale: Scales over multiple summarization tasks and large clinical corpora; sequence-input scaling not addressed.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides reader-study and automatic-metric protocols transferable to validating sequence-conditioned instruction tuning.

candidates.paper_title: BioMistral: A Collection of Open-Source Pretrained Large Language Models for Medical Domains

useful_outcomes.rationale: Open biomedical LLM with strong instruction-following on PubMedQA/MedQA; sequence conditioning not central.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open weights and instruction-tuning code immediately usable; multilingual evaluation included.

plausible.rationale: Mistral-based methodology is sound and reproducibly described.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrated at 7B scale on PubMed Central; recipe scales further.

how_to_validate.rationale: Standard biomedical QA benchmarks; sequence-grounded reasoning not specifically evaluated.

candidates.paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science

useful_outcomes.rationale: Galactica trained on scientific corpora handles protein/SMILES sequences in prompts; partial sequence-conditioned reasoning demonstrated.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open scientific LLM with citation/sequence handling; recipe adaptable, though model checkpoint controversial.

plausible.rationale: Methodology sound; limitations widely documented around hallucination on factual prompts.

scalable.rationale: Trained on 100B+ tokens of scientific text; scaling to literature-scale demonstrated.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides scientific QA and reasoning benchmarks usable as evaluation surface for sequence-aware variants.

candidates.paper_title: BioGPT: generative pre-trained transformer for biomedical text generation and mining

useful_outcomes.rationale: BioGPT pretrained on biomedical literature; not instruction-tuned on sequence inputs and lacks DNA/RNA conditioning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Strong open biomedical LLM checkpoint that teams routinely instruction-tune downstream for sequence-aware tasks.

plausible.rationale: GPT-style biomedical pretraining is sound and well validated on text generation/relation extraction.

scalable.rationale: Trained at literature scale on PubMed; architecture scales to larger sequence-aware variants.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides BC5CDR/KD-DTI/end-to-end benchmarks usable as base evaluation for instruction-tuned descendants.

candidates.paper_title: BloombergGPT: A Large Language Model for Finance

useful_outcomes.rationale: Domain LLM for finance, not biology; instruction-following demonstrated but unrelated to DNA/RNA sequences.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Recipe for domain-specialized LLM training is generally adoptable; cited as architectural blueprint.

plausible.rationale: 50B-parameter training methodology is well documented and sound.

scalable.rationale: Demonstrates literature-scale text training; transferable scaling lessons.

how_to_validate.rationale: Validation focuses on financial benchmarks; minimal transfer to sequence-grounded reasoning evaluation.

candidates.paper_title: Opportunities and challenges for ChatGPT and large language models in biomedicine and health

useful_outcomes.rationale: Surveys ChatGPT applications in biomedicine; sequence-conditioned instruction tuning only briefly discussed.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Identifies use cases and pitfalls; minimal directly adoptable methodology.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable peer-reviewed survey methodology; opinion-heavy in places.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling considerations; not an engineering contribution.

how_to_validate.rationale: Reviews evaluation frameworks for biomedical LLMs that partially transfer to sequence tasks.

candidates.paper_title: A Comprehensive Overview of Large Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Comprehensive overview discusses instruction tuning generally; minimal sequence-conditioning content.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful tutorial-style reference for teams new to LLMs but offers no concrete bio-sequence recipe.

plausible.rationale: Aggregates established LLM techniques accurately; not an empirical contribution.

scalable.rationale: Surveys scalable methods but does not demonstrate scaling itself.

how_to_validate.rationale: Catalogs benchmarks but not specific to sequence-grounded instruction following.

multimodal-text-conditioned-generation.claim_text: Text-conditioned sequence generation — describing a desired biological function, regulatory context, or therapeutic target in natural language and having the model propose candidate DNA / RNA sequences — turns LLM-style natural-language interfaces into a primitive of biological sequence design.

candidates.paper_title: A Text-guided Protein Design Framework

useful_outcomes.rationale: ProteinDT achieves >90% text-guided protein generation accuracy and best zero-shot text-guided editing; direct strong evidence for the claim.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open code, 441K text-protein dataset (SwissProtCLAP), and three-stage recipe directly portable to DNA/RNA.

plausible.rationale: Methodology (CLAP alignment + facilitator + decoder) is sound and validated across three challenging tasks.

scalable.rationale: SwissProt-scale demonstration; framework generalizable but genome-scale text-DNA pairs not yet shown.

how_to_validate.rationale: Quantitative protocols (accuracy, hit ratio, property prediction) directly transferable to text-conditioned DNA generation.

candidates.paper_title: A multimodal conversational agent for DNA, RNA and protein tasks

useful_outcomes.rationale: ChatNT handles English questions about DNA/RNA/protein and can be prompted to produce structured answers; sequence-out generation lighter than seq-in QA.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Published, peer-reviewed multimodal conversational agent with curated instruction set; DNA/RNA support immediately useful.

plausible.rationale: Solid empirical results on Nucleotide Transformer benchmark; methodology peer-reviewed at NMI.

scalable.rationale: Built on nucleotide-transformer foundation handling long DNA contexts; scales across species and tissues.

how_to_validate.rationale: Provides perplexity calibration and curated instruction tasks transferable as validation protocol.

candidates.paper_title: ChatNT: A Multimodal Conversational Agent for DNA, RNA and Protein Tasks

useful_outcomes.rationale: Preprint version of ChatNT; demonstrates multimodal conversational handling of DNA/RNA/protein with curated instruction tasks.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open methodology and benchmark; widely cited blueprint for text-conditioned biological generation interfaces.

plausible.rationale: Empirical results match the NMI version; methodology sound though preprint stage.

scalable.rationale: Same nucleotide-transformer backbone scales across DNA/RNA/protein and species.

how_to_validate.rationale: Same calibration and instruction benchmark transfers.

candidates.paper_title: CARE: a Benchmark Suite for the Classification and Retrieval of Enzymes

useful_outcomes.rationale: Provides retrieval task linking chemical reactions (text-like) to enzyme EC numbers; partial text-conditioned protein retrieval evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open benchmark suite directly usable to evaluate text-conditioned generation/retrieval models.

plausible.rationale: Carefully designed train-test splits with OOD evaluation; sound benchmark methodology.

scalable.rationale: Enzyme-level scale; framework extensible to broader sequence generation tasks.

how_to_validate.rationale: Explicit OOD splits and dual classification/retrieval tasks form an excellent validation template.

candidates.paper_title: Multi-modal Transfer Learning between Biological Foundation Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: IsoFormer connects DNA/RNA/protein encoders for transcript expression prediction; multimodal but not text-conditioned generation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Open model and recipe useful for multimodal genomic teams; lacks text modality input.

plausible.rationale: Sound encoder-fusion architecture with clear empirical wins on transcript expression.

scalable.rationale: Built on Nucleotide-Transformer-class encoders; scales to genome-level inputs.

how_to_validate.rationale: Differential transcript expression validation transferable indirectly to text-conditioned generation.

candidates.paper_title: Scientific Large Language Models: A Survey on Biological & Chemical Domains

useful_outcomes.rationale: Survey reviews text-conditioned sequence generation systems across protein/molecule/genomic domains; not a primary contribution.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful taxonomy and citations for teams building text-to-sequence generators; no checkpoint released.

plausible.rationale: Comprehensive recent survey with sound methodology.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling regimes; itself does not scale anything.

how_to_validate.rationale: Aggregates validation patterns from text-conditioned generative biology that transfer.

candidates.paper_title: Generative artificial intelligence for de novo protein design

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reviews generative protein design including text-conditioned approaches; surveys ~20% experimental success rates.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Useful framework for organizing text-to-sequence design pipelines; no checkpoint.

plausible.rationale: Recent peer-style review with field-wide perspective; methodology sound for survey.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling issues without empirical demonstration.

how_to_validate.rationale: Highlights in silico vs experimental metrics, directly relevant to validating text-conditioned DNA/RNA design.

candidates.paper_title: Learning the Language of Protein Structure

useful_outcomes.rationale: Tokenizes 3D protein structure for GPT-style generation; demonstrates language-of-structure design but no text conditioning.

immediate_benefit.rationale: VQ-VAE codebook approach is portable to multimodal pipelines targeting DNA/RNA.

plausible.rationale: Sound VQ-VAE methodology with reasonable RMSD reconstructions and GPT generation.

scalable.rationale: Codebook size 4K-64K demonstrates scalability of the discretization approach.

how_to_validate.rationale: Generation quality and RMSD metrics transfer in spirit to multimodal sequence design validation.

candidates.paper_title: Controllable Protein Design with Language Models

useful_outcomes.rationale: Argues for control-tag conditioned protein language models including function/compartment tags; partial text-conditioning evidence.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Position/review paper rather than a released model; conceptual recipe only.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable extrapolation from established CTRL-style conditioning; not empirically validated here.

scalable.rationale: Conceptually scalable; no empirical scaling demonstration.

how_to_validate.rationale: Sketches validation strategies but lacks concrete benchmarks.

candidates.paper_title: Large Language Models in Drug Discovery and Development: From Disease Mechanisms to Clinical Trials

useful_outcomes.rationale: Reviews LLMs in drug discovery with brief coverage of text-conditioned molecule generation; little on DNA/RNA generation.

immediate_benefit.rationale: Provides reference catalog rather than concrete generation recipes.

plausible.rationale: Reasonable review of an emerging area.

scalable.rationale: Discusses scaling generally.

how_to_validate.rationale: Survey-level discussion of evaluation; partial transfer to text-to-sequence validation.
